sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 1 action in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: SPACE.
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 41, level 1.
Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT, SPACE.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current world model from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE WORLD MODEL.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Working world model carried from earlier turns:
- World model: Player at [32-35, 32-34] (4x3), directly above bottom-center box at [36-39, 32-35]. Player's bottom edge (row 35) touches box's top edge (row 36). Columns partially overlap (32-34 vs 32-35). Left box at [28-31, 16-19] still needs activation.
- Plan: Try SPACE from current position (above the box) to see if it activates. If not, move to the side.
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
If you include assistant text before a tool call, keep it short and use it to update the world model. Helpful optional prefixes are `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Action model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, and `Cross-level notes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 40 of about 71 target actions so far.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting! The SPACE didn't activate the bottom-center box. Instead:
- The white box at [32-35, 35-39] changed to dark gray (G) at [32-35, 36-39]
- The bottom-center box at [36-39, 32-35] is still charcoal (c)
- The player is still at [32-35, 32-34]
So SPACE from above the box didn't work. The white box that was previously activated turned back to dark gray (G) and shifted position. This suggests SPACE has a different effect depending on position relative to the target box.
Wait, let me reconsider. The white box was at [32-35, 35-39] before this SPACE. After SPACE, it's now at [32-35, 36-39] with color G (dark gray). It shifted right by 1 column and changed from white to dark gray.
Also, the bottom-right corner changed: color=c at rows [63-63], cols [51-63], px=13 (was [52-63], px=12 before). The dark bar at the bottom shifted.
So SPACE from above the box didn't activate it. Instead, it affected the previously activated box (turned it from white back to dark gray).
This means the activation rule is more specific. Let me reconsider:
1. First activation: player at [29-31, 44-47], box at [24-27, 44-47]. Player BELOW box, same columns. Box turned white.
2. Second activation: player at [32-35, 32-34], box at [32-35, 36-39]. Player LEFT of box, same rows. Box turned white.
3. Failed attempt: player at [32-35, 32-34], box at [36-39, 32-35]. Player ABOVE box, overlapping columns. Box did NOT turn white. Instead, the previously activated box turned back to dark gray.
So the activation rule seems to be: player needs to be BELOW or to the SIDE (left/right) of the box, at the same row or column range. Being ABOVE doesn't work.
For the bottom-center box at [36-39, 32-35]:
- I need to be BELOW it (rows 40+, cols 32-35) or to the LEFT (rows 36-39, cols <32) or to the RIGHT (rows 36-39, cols >35)
- Currently I'm ABOVE it
For the left box at [28-31, 16-19]:
- I need to be BELOW it (rows 32+, cols 16-19) or to the LEFT (rows 28-31, cols <16) or to the RIGHT (rows 28-31, cols >19)
Let me plan:
1. First, move DOWN to get below the bottom-center box
2. Position at rows 40-43, cols 32-35 (below the box)
3. Press SPACE to activate it
4. Then navigate to the left box and activate it
But wait, I also need to worry about the previously activated box turning back to dark gray. The white box at [32-35, 35-39] turned to dark gray at [32-35, 36-39] after my failed SPACE. Does this mean I need to re-activate it?
Actually, looking more carefully, the box at [32-35, 36-39] is now color G (dark gray), not W (white). So the activation was "undone" or "reversed" by the failed SPACE. This is important - I need to be careful not to waste activations.
Let me focus on activating the remaining boxes. I'll start with the bottom-center box.
Current player: [32-35, 32-34] (4x3)
Target: bottom-center box at [36-39, 32-35]
Need to be: BELOW the box at rows 40-43, cols 32-35
To get there:
1. Move DOWN to get to rows 36-39 (but this overlaps with the box)
2. Move DOWN again to get to rows 40-43
But moving DOWN changes the player shape. Currently 4x3 (from RIGHT movement). Moving DOWN would keep it 4x3 (since DOWN is vertical, and the last movement was horizontal... wait, I need to re-examine the shape rules).
Actually, let me re-examine:
- Initial: 3x4 (horizontal)
- After moving UP: 3x4 (still horizontal)
- After moving RIGHT: 4x3 (vertical)
- After moving LEFT: 4x3 (still vertical)
So the rule seems to be:
- Moving UP/DOWN → 3x4 (horizontal)
- Moving LEFT/RIGHT → 4x3 (vertical)
Wait, but after moving UP, the player was 3x4. Then after moving RIGHT, it became 4x3. So:
- UP/DOWN → 3x4
- LEFT/RIGHT → 4x3
To activate the bottom-center box from below, I need the player at rows 40-43, cols 32-35. If I move DOWN, the player becomes 3x4 (3 rows, 4 cols). So the player would be at rows 40-42, cols 32-35. That's 3 rows, which matches the 3x4 shape.
Let me try:
1. DOWN → player at rows 36-38, cols 32-35 (3x4) - but this overlaps with the box at rows 36-39
2. DOWN again → player at rows 40-42, cols 32-35 (3x4) - below the box
Wait, but the player moves in jumps. Let me check the movement amounts:
- UP: 40→37→33 (3 rows per UP)
- DOWN: should be similar
If I'm at rows 32-35 and move DOWN:
- First DOWN: rows 35-38 (if moving 3 rows)
- Second DOWN: rows 38-41
Hmm, but the player is 4 rows tall (32-35), so moving DOWN by 3 rows would put it at 35-38. But the box is at 36-39, so the player would overlap with the box.
Actually, I think the player can't overlap with boxes. Let me just try moving DOWN and see what happens.
Actually wait, I realize I should first check if the previously activated box needs to be re-activated. The box at [32-35, 36-39] is now G (dark gray), not W (white). If I need all boxes to be white to complete the level, then I need to re-activate it.
But let me first focus on understanding the movement and getting to the bottom-center box. Let me try DOWN.

sections.content:
World model: SPACE from above didn't activate the bottom-center box. Instead, the previously activated white box turned back to dark gray (G). Activation requires player to be BELOW or to the SIDE of the box, not ABOVE. Player at [32-35, 32-34] (4x3). Need to activate: bottom-center [36-39, 32-35] and left [28-31, 16-19]. May need to re-activate the box at [32-35, 36-39].
Plan: Move DOWN to get below the bottom-center box, then SPACE to activate it.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Player at 32-35, 32-34 (4x3)
# Try DOWN to get below the bottom-center box

result = action(['DOWN'])
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        h = max(rows) - min(rows) + 1
        w = max(cols) - min(cols) + 1
        print(f"After DOWN: rows [{min(rows)}-{max(rows)}], cols [{min(cols)}-{max(cols)}], shape {h}x{w}")

# Check all boxes
print("\nAll boxes:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ['c', 'G', 'W'] and n['pixels'] >= 8:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  color={n['color']}, rows [{min(rows)}-{max(rows)}], cols [{min(cols)}-{max(cols)}]")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.content:

  color=c, rows [28-31], cols [16-19]
  color=c, rows [32-35], cols [36-39]
  color=G, rows [36-39], cols [32-35]
  color=c, rows [63-63], cols [51-63]
